title: J-ICS reform key driver of steady build-up in Japan life insurers’ reinsurance usage
title_original: J-ICS reform key driver of steady build-up in Japan life insurers’ reinsurance usage
summary: A new AM Best report has revealed that Japan’s life insurers have increasingly relied on reinsurance in recent years, driven largely by the segment preparing for the new J-ICS solvency regime, which took effect at the end of March 2026. Cynthia Ang, senior industry research analyst, AM Best, added, “Japanese life insurers have been increasingly using asset-intensive reinsurance to transfer investment, longevity and insurance risks from capital-intensive annuity and long-term life insurance blocks to third-party reinsurers ahead of the implementation of J-ICS. “The maturity and size of Japan’s life/annuity insurance market make it an attractive opportunity for reinsurers providing asset-intensive reinsurance solutions.” Under the J-ICS, the new economic value-based solvency ratio will reportedly be more sensitive to fluctuations in interest rates, lapses, asset-liability management mismatches and longevity/mortality risks.

title: Shadow Accounting in Insurance Compensation
title_original: Shadow Accounting in Insurance Compensation
summary: Why shadow accounting happens in insurance Insurance compensation is uniquely difficult to manage because it sits at the intersection of money, relationships, compliance, and strategy. But in sales and compensation environments, shadow accounting usually looks less formal, consisting of spreadsheets, offline trackers, manually maintained commission logs, and side calculations used to validate or challenge official payout information. In insurance distribution, shadow accounting often appears when producers, managers, or back-office teams cannot easily see how commissions were calculated, whether adjustments were applied, or how hierarchy and eligibility changes affected payment.
